mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and signals monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose to 0.41 in Nov 2016 amid Dec 2016 FOMC rate hike
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following rising-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls to or below 4.7% while still declining, the labor market is judged to be at or beyond full employment, raising wage-inflation and Fed-tightening expectations that depress S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE <= 4.7% and falling relative to 3-month prior value
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE revised value 4.7% as of Nov 2016, near cycle lows
failure_condition: S&P 500 20d forward returns following triggered low-UNRATE episodes are positive in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in RSAFS reflects robust consumer spending momentum that feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value 465513 million in Nov 2016 showing continued retail growth
failure_condition: Correlation between RSAFS MoM change and subsequent 5d SPX returns falls below 0.03 Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in INDPRO below 0% signals contraction in industrial output consistent with manufacturing recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 98.3491 in Nov 2016 reflecting soft industrial output
failure_condition: S&P 500 60d forward returns during INDPRO YoY-negative episodes are positive more than 55% of the time, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in PAYEMS growth and decline in UNRATE indicates a jointly overheating labor market that raises inflation and Fed-hike odds beyond what either series signals alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > prior 6-month average AND UNRATE falling
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145128 thousand and UNRATE at 4.6-4.7% in late 2016 indicating tight labor market
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS-UNRATE tightening signal fails to produce significantly lower 20d forward returns than the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising FEDFUNDS combined with falling HOUST indicates tightening credit conditions transmitting into interest-rate-sensitive housing activity, a leading indicator of broader demand weakness that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over 3 months AND HOUST declining over same window
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1313-1323 thousand units in Oct 2016 amid rising rate expectations
failure_condition: Joint FEDFUNDS-up/HOUST-down regime fails to show significantly negative 60d SPX returns relative to unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During an active monetary tightening cycle, acceleration in CPIAUCSL amplifies market expectations of faster Fed rate hikes, causing sharper negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days than the same inflation signal would produce in a neutral or easing regime.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change accelerates while FEDFUNDS is on a rising trajectory
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage 242.348 in Nov 2016 with Fed hiking in Dec 2016
failure_condition: 20d SPX returns following CPI acceleration during tightening regimes are not significantly more negative than CPI acceleration during easing/neutral regimes (p > 0.05), invalidating the regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in HOUST reflects strengthening housing construction activity and consumer confidence, a leading indicator of broad economic momentum that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value 1323 thousand units in Oct 2016 near post-recession highs
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST trend and subsequent 5d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive HOUST trend periods do not show significantly higher returns than baseline (p > 0.05).
